frameworks.name: neuroeconomic subjective value encoding
frameworks.one_line: Neural correlates of preference strength index subjective value of rewards

frameworks.name: cue-reactivity / incentive salience
frameworks.one_line: Drug cues modulate value signals in substance use contexts

core_question: How does the brain encode subjective value for cannabis versus non-drug rewards, and how do cues modulate this encoding in regular cannabis users?
key_assumptions: Subjective value can be operationalized as neural correlates of preference strength
vmPFC, ventral striatum, dPCC are canonical SV-encoding regions
Repeated binary choices between drug puffs and money index relative value
manipulations: Reward type: cannabis puffs vs snacks
Cue exposure: neutral vs reward-matched cues
key_findings: Choice behavior was equivalent between cannabis and snack conditions
SV signals were observed for cannabis but not snacks in vmPFC, ventral striatum, dPCC
vmPFC SV encoding was stronger for cannabis than snacks
Cue impact on dPCC SV signals was moderated by reward type
interpretations: Cannabis recruits canonical value-encoding circuitry more robustly than non-drug rewards in regular users
Cue-driven modulation of value signals is reward-type specific in posterior cingulate
